title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) at home with our easy, authentic recipe. Perfect for a delicious Korean meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon
Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon Results
Serving Suggestions for Your Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly cooked Haemul Pajeon, a savory Korean seafood pancake, garnished with green onions and served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a perfect Haemul Pajeon, a crispy and flavorful Korean seafood pancake, made fresh at home.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, packed with an array of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This iconic dish offers a delightful textural contrast and rich umami flavor, making it a perfect appetizer or light meal for any occasion. It is often served with a tangy soy-based dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your assorted seafood, cutting larger pieces like squid into bite-sized rings or strips. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and optional red chili. Set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, cold water, egg, and salt until a smooth batter forms. Be careful not to overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood and sliced onion to the batter. Gently stir to ensure all ingredients are coated. Do not add the green onions to the batter yet, they will be placed directly in the pan.

instructions.title: Heat Pan and Arrange Green Onions
instructions.text: Heat 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once the oil is hot, arrange a layer of green onion pieces neatly across the bottom of the pan. This creates the signature green onion base.

instructions.title: Pour Batter and Cook First Side
instructions.text: Carefully pour half of the seafood and batter mixture over the green onions, spreading it evenly to cover the pan. Press down gently with a spatula to ensure the green onions are submerged. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the edges are golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Cook Second Side
instructions.text: Drizzle another 1-2 tablespoons of oil around the edges of the pancake. Using a large spatula, carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until the second side is golden brown and cooked through. Repeat for the second pancake if making two.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce and Serve
instructions.text: While the pancake cooks, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and toasted sesame seeds for the dipping sauce. Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a serving plate, slice into wedges, garnish with sliced red chili if desired, and serve immediately with the dipping sauce.

tips: Use very cold water for the batter to help create a crispier pancake. You can even add an ice cube to the water.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook in batches if your pan isn't large enough to ensure even cooking and crispiness.
For extra crunch, sprinkle a small amount of extra flour or cornstarch directly onto the green onions in the pan before pouring the batter.
article:
Mastering the Art of Haemul Pajeon: Your Ultimate Korean Seafood Pancake Guide

Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean seafood pancake, is a culinary masterpiece that perfectly balances crispy edges with a soft, savory interior, brimming with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. More than just a dish, it’s a celebration of flavors and textures, often enjoyed with friends and family, especially alongside a refreshing glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) on a rainy day. If you’ve ever savored its delightful taste at a Korean restaurant, you know the craving is real. But did you know this iconic dish is surprisingly accessible to make at home? This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to create an authentic and delicious Haemul Pajeon, from selecting the right ingredients to mastering the perfect flip. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your loved ones with this incredible Haemul Pajeon Recipe.

## What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?

Haemul Pajeon translates literally to "seafood green onion pancake," and that name perfectly encapsulates its essence. It's a type of jeon, a general term for Korean savory pancakes made from various ingredients mixed with a flour-based batter and pan-fried. What sets Haemul Pajeon apart is its generous inclusion of fresh seafood – typically shrimp, squid, and mussels – combined with an abundance of green onions (scallions), all bound together in a light, flavorful batter. Unlike thinner, crepe-like pancakes, Haemul Pajeon boasts a satisfying thickness and a wonderful textural contrast: a golden-brown, crispy exterior yielding to a tender, chewy interior. The green onions aren't just a garnish; they are a core component, providing a sweet, pungent flavor that harmonizes beautifully with the briny seafood. It’s a dish that embodies comfort, flavor, and the vibrant spirit of Korean cuisine, making it a staple at Korean eateries and home kitchens alike.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon begins with fresh, quality ingredients. Here's a breakdown of what you'll need for this delightful Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

For the Pancake Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or a mix of all-purpose and rice flour for extra crispiness)
*   1/2 cup Korean pancake mix (buchim garu) (optional, but enhances flavor and texture)
*   1 1/2 cups cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For the Fillings:
*   2 cups green onions (scallions), cut into 2-3 inch pieces
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood (shrimp, squid rings, mussels, oysters), cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces
*   1/4 cup onion, thinly sliced (optional)
*   1-2 fresh red or green chili peppers, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)

For Pan-Frying:
*   4-5 tablespoons vegetable oil (or any neutral cooking oil)

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)
*   Pinch of red pepper flakes (gochugaru) (optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon

Follow these steps to bring your Haemul Pajeon Recipe to life:

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce**: In a small bowl, combine all dipping sauce ingredients. Stir well and set aside. This can be made ahead of time.
2.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**: Clean and pat dry all seafood. Cut larger pieces of shrimp or squid into bite-sized portions. Cut green onions into 2-3 inch lengths. If using, thinly slice the onion and chili peppers.
3.  **Make the Batter**: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, Korean pancake mix (if using), salt, and black pepper. Gradually add the cold water, whisking constantly until a smooth batter forms. It should be relatively thin, similar to a crepe batter, but slightly thicker. Whisk in the egg until fully incorporated. Avoid overmixing, as this can make the pancake tough.
4.  **Combine Fillings with Batter (Optional Method)**: You can either add all the seafood and green onions directly into the batter and mix gently, or follow the traditional layering method for a more visually appealing pancake. For the latter, proceed to step 5.
5.  **Heat the Pan**: Place a large non-stick skillet (about 10-12 inches) over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil and swirl to coat the bottom evenly.
6.  **Assemble and Cook the Pancake (Layering Method)**:
    *   Once the oil is hot (it should shimmer), arrange about half of the green onion pieces in a single layer on the bottom of the pan.
    *   Evenly distribute about half of the mixed seafood over the green onions. Add any sliced onions or chili peppers.
    *   Pour about half of the batter over the seafood and green onions, ensuring it spreads evenly to cover all ingredients and connect them. Use a spatula to gently press down on the ingredients.
    *   Cook for 5-7 minutes until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the batter appears mostly set.
7.  **Flip the Pancake**: Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully slide a large, flat spatula underneath the pancake. With a swift, confident motion, flip the pancake over.
8.  **Cook the Second Side**: Continue cooking for another 4-6 minutes, pressing down occasionally with the spatula, until the second side is golden brown and crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked.
9.  **Serve**: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Cut into wedge-shaped pieces or squares. Repeat the process for the second pancake. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon Results

Achieving that perfect balance of crispiness and tenderness in your Haemul Pajeon requires a few expert tricks:

*   **Cold Batter is Key**: Using very cold water (or even ice water) for your batter helps prevent gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crispier pancake. Some even add a few ice cubes to the batter while it rests.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil**: Haemul Pajeon isn't meant to be a low-fat dish. Generous amounts of oil are crucial for achieving that irresistible crispy exterior. Don't be afraid to add more around the edges before flipping.
*   **High Heat to Start, Then Adjust**: Begin with medium-high heat to get a good sizzle and crisp start, then you might slightly reduce it to medium to ensure the inside cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Rest the Batter**: Let your batter rest for 10-15 minutes before cooking. This allows the flour to fully hydrate, resulting in a smoother, more consistent batter and a tender pancake.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry**: Excess moisture from the seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat your shrimp, squid, and mussels thoroughly dry with paper towels before adding them to the batter or pan.
*   **The Confident Flip**: Flipping a large pancake can be intimidating. Ensure your pan is well-oiled, and use a large, sturdy spatula. A quick, decisive flip is better than a hesitant one. Alternatively, you can slide the pancake onto a plate, then invert the pan over the plate and flip it back into the pan.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Haemul Pajeon

Haemul Pajeon is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways:

*   **As an Appetizer**: Cut into smaller squares or wedges, it makes a fantastic starter for any Korean meal.
*   **As a Main Dish**: Paired with a simple side of kimchi and rice, it can easily be a satisfying light meal.
*   **With Makgeolli**: Traditionally, Haemul Pajeon is the quintessential pairing for makgeolli, especially on a rainy day. The slightly sweet, tangy notes of the rice wine cut through the richness of the pancake beautifully.
*   **With Beer or Soju**: If makgeolli isn't your preference, a cold beer or a shot of soju also makes an excellent companion.
*   **The Dipping Sauce is Essential**: Never forget the dipping sauce! Its savory, tangy, and slightly spicy notes provide a crucial counterpoint to the rich pancake.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon Recipe is fantastic, don't hesitate to experiment and customize it to your liking:

*   **Different Seafood**: Feel free to use your favorite seafood. Clams, oysters, crab meat, or even flaky white fish can be excellent additions. Just ensure they are cut into small, quick-cooking pieces.
*   **Add More Vegetables**: Beyond green onions, you can incorporate other vegetables like shredded carrots, bell peppers, zucchini, or even kimchi for a kimchi pajeon twist. Just make sure to squeeze out excess moisture from watery vegetables.
*   **Mushrooms**: Sliced shiitake or oyster mushrooms add an earthy depth of flavor and a lovely texture.
*   **Spicy Kick**: For those who love heat, increase the amount of fresh chili peppers or add a tablespoon of gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the batter.
*   **Vegetarian Pajeon (Yachaejeon)**: Omit the seafood entirely and focus on a variety of vegetables like carrots, zucchini, onions, mushrooms, and bell peppers for a delicious vegetarian pancake. This is a great alternative to the traditional Haemul Pajeon Recipe.
*   **Cheese Pajeon**: For a modern twist, sprinkle some shredded mozzarella or cheddar cheese over the pancake during the last few minutes of cooking for a gooey, savory treat.

Bringing the vibrant flavors of Korea into your kitchen has never been easier or more rewarding. This detailed Haemul Pajeon Recipe provides all the guidance you need to create a truly authentic and delectable Korean seafood pancake. Whether you're a seasoned cook or a kitchen novice, follow these steps, embrace the tips, and prepare to delight in the crispy, savory goodness of Haemul Pajeon. Enjoy your culinary journey!
